architecture.summary: TP-Transformer enriches the standard Transformer encoder-decoder with Tensor Product Representations (TPR) by factorizing each token's representation into two components at every layer: a role vector (encoding structural/syntactic information) computed via attention over a learned dictionary of role embeddings, and a filler vector (the multi-head attention output encoding semantic content). These are bound via Hadamard product and combined with residual connections before passing through feed-forward layers. This introduces a structural inductive bias that encourages discrete role spaces and disentangled syntax/semantics.
trained_components: role embeddings dictionary (50 embeddings of dimension 64 per layer)
linear projection for role attention scores

biblio.title: Enriching Transformers with Structured Tensor-Product Representations for Abstractive Summarization

claims.statement: On XSum with beam search (4), TP-Transformer (TPT-d) achieves ROUGE-1/ROUGE-2/ROUGE-L/METEOR of 35.84/14.06/28.40/25.79, outperforming standard Transformer at 34.46/12.97/27.21/24.42
On Wikihow with beam search (4), TP-Transformer achieves ROUGE-1/ROUGE-2/ROUGE-L/METEOR of 36.70/13.75/27.36/22.53, outperforming Transformer at 35.91/13.49/27.01/21.57
On Arxiv with beam search (4), TP-Transformer achieves ROUGE-1/ROUGE-2/ROUGE-L/METEOR of 38.36/11.43/34.29/23.61, outperforming Transformer at 38.01/11.33/34.02/23.19
On CNN/Daily Mail with beam search (4), TP-Transformer achieves 41.01/18.38/37.91/34.34, achieving competitive performance with Transformer at 41.22/18.70/38.09/34.50
On XSum human evaluation (120 samples), TP-Transformer wins 52 out of 120 on overall preference versus Transformer's 48, with TP-Transformer winning 47 samples on grammar versus Transformer's 39
More than 90% of learned role attention distributions have a maximum score larger than 0.98, indicating that role vectors reside in a highly discrete space and induce syntactic structures

Probing decoder role vectors on POS tagging shows they encode grammatical information, while filler vectors represent semantics; TPR representations outperform both roles and fillers alone at every layer

On CNN/Daily Mail, the least abstractive dataset, TP-Transformer achieves only competitive performance with standard Transformer, with the largest advantages observed on more abstractive datasets (XSum, Wikihow)
